summary: Matched a large batch of gm_16F1 game-mode functions, primarily around unlock/check bookkeeping, challenger state, one-player progress, and post-match condition handling. The decomp.dev report recorded +4120 matched bytes, 26 newly matched gm_16F1 functions, and 3 additional functions improved to about 99% match. The PR also tightened several headers and shared types so other gm modules could stop using raw UNK_T/u8 indexing for lbl_8046DBD8 and related game-mode data.

key_files.role: Main matching work; replaced many placeholder /// #function stubs with C implementations and matching helpers/static tables.
Updated prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS/UNK_T to more specific argument and return types for matched functions.
Added and refined shared structs, including lbl_8046DBD8_t, gmm_retval_ED98, gmm_retval_EDBC, and Unk1PData field typing.
Changed static lbl_8046DBD8 storage from raw UNK_T array to lbl_8046DBD8_t.
Reused lbl_8046DBD8_t instead of an anonymous local struct.
Replaced u8 pointer indexing into gm_801736DC result with typed lbl_8046DBD8_t field access.
Refined gmMainLib_8015D804 return type to u32*.
Header counterpart for gmMainLib_8015D804 return type refinement.
Changed gm_80181A24 return type from u16 to u32.
Header counterpart for gm_80181A24 return type refinement.
Adjusted un_803048C0 signature/typing used by unlock checks.

what_changed: Implemented 26 newly matching functions in main/melee/gm/gm_16F1 according to the decomp.dev bot report: gm_801729EC, gm_8017390C, gm_80173DE4, fn_80172FAC, gm_80172898, fn_80173510, fn_80173834, gm_80173C70, gm_80172CC0, gm_80173BC4, gm_80173D3C, gm_80172DD4, gm_80173B30, gm_80173AA4, gm_8017297C, gm_80172D78, fn_8017232C, fn_80172380, fn_801723D4, gm_8017219C, gm_801737E8_OnLoad, gm_801720F8, gm_801720B4, gm_80172140, gm_80172C44, and gm_80172174.
Improved three more gm_16F1 functions without reaching full match in the bot report: fn_80173098 to 99.39%, gm_80173754 to 99.88%, and gm_801736E8 to 99.74%.
Added lbtime include usage in gm_16F1.c and used lbTime_8000AFBC, lbTime_8000AEE4, and lbTime_8000AEC8 for time/progress comparisons.
Recovered static lookup data used by gm_16F1 logic, including lbl_803B7AD0, lbl_803D6450, and lbl_803D646C local structs/tables.

Replaced magic character and mode values in touched code with named constants such as CHKIND_NONE, CKIND_MEWTWO, CKIND_ZELDA, CKIND_SEAK, CKIND_GAMEWATCH, MJ_CLASSIC, MJ_ADVENTURE, MJ_ALLSTAR, MJ_CHALLENGER_APPROACH, MJ_15MIN_VS, MJ_100MAN_VS, MJ_ENDLESS_VS, and MJ_CRUEL_VS where shown in the diff.
Created lbl_8046DBD8_t in gm/types.h and used it for gm_801736DC, gm_801736E8, gm_80173754, gm_801737D8, gm_801737E8_OnLoad, gm_1BFA.c, and gmmovieend.c.

Refined several prototypes: gm_80172898 now takes u16 and returns int, gm_80172CC0 takes u8 arguments, gm_80172DD4 takes u32, gm_801736DC returns lbl_8046DBD8_t*, fn_80173834 has named ckind/major/bool-like parameters, gm_80173B30 takes u32, gm_80173C70 uses u32-sized arguments, and gm_80173D3C takes int.
Updated gmmain_lib accessors and structs to support typed field access, notably gmMainLib_8015D804 returning u32* and gmm_retval_EDBC exposing xB0[0x19] and x114[0x19].
smart_moves: Recovered and centralized lbl_8046DBD8_t instead of leaving several modules to reinterpret the same data as UNK_T, u8*, or anonymous structs. This made gm_1BFA.c and gmmovieend.c cleaner and reduced duplicated layout guesses.
Used small static inline helpers around lookup-table scans, which let the matched code express table-driven unlock logic without over-expanding call structure.
Applied #pragma dont_inline around functions where inlining decisions matter for matching, including gm_80172898, gm_80173754, and gm_8017390C in the diff excerpt.
Kept a targeted TODO on gm_80172174 noting that gmMainLib_8015CCE4 returns a still-unknown 0x4D8-sized struct, while still matching by using memzero(..., 0x4D8).
Converted raw numeric sentinels like 0x21 in gm_80173224 to CHKIND_NONE, improving readability while preserving behavior.
Used typed pointer return values from gmMainLib_8015D804 so assignments like *tmp = lbTime_8000AFBC() or *tmp = 0 compile naturally without repeated casts.
Recovered exact field widths where matching depended on them, such as Unk1PData.xC.x20 changing to u32 and gm_80181A24 returning u32 rather than u16.
decomp_lessons: When a global scratch/static object is accessed across multiple gm files, promote it into a shared named struct once enough fields are known; this avoids divergent anonymous definitions and makes downstream matching easier.
Header prototypes are part of matching work: refining argument widths and return types can remove casts, fix call ABI assumptions, and make other files match with less local noise.

Prefer project enum/constant names for known character IDs and minor/major scene IDs after behavior is understood; constants like CKIND_ZELDA and MJ_ALLSTAR make unlock rules searchable and less error-prone.
For table-driven game-mode logic, reconstructing static table element structs and sentinel/ARRAY_SIZE loops is often the clearest path to a match.

Do not force premature complete struct recovery when evidence is incomplete; a documented memzero size can be acceptable if a future struct is explicitly called out, as with the 0x4D8 gmMainLib_8015CCE4 TODO.
Use decomp pragmas and small inlines deliberately to match compiler behavior rather than flattening all logic into large functions.
Progress/unlock routines frequently compare stored counters and times, so recovering u32 versus signed/int field types matters for both matching and semantic clarity.
naming_conventions: Unresolved functions kept address-based names such as gm_801729EC, fn_80172FAC, and gmMainLib_8015D804, matching existing project style.
Recovered anonymous/static data structs were named after labels with _t suffixes, e.g. lbl_8046DBD8_t, lbl_803B7AD0_t, lbl_803D6450_t, and lbl_803D646C_t.
Temporary variables in matched functions used register-derived names where useful, such as temp_r31, temp_r30, var_r29, and var_r31, consistent with decomp practice.
Character-kind variables were clarified as ckind/c_kind where the semantic role was known.
Game-mode constants used project-style CKIND_* and MJ_* names instead of raw literals in several touched regions.
Function prototypes retained address comments in headers, e.g. /* 172898 */ and /* 1736DC */, preserving searchable symbol/address mapping.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: #pragma push / #pragma dont_inline on / #pragma pop was used around selected functions to control compiler inlining and preserve expected code shape.
PAD_STACK(8) appears in gm_80172DD4, indicating a stack-layout tactic for matching.
Static inline helper functions such as inline3, inline2, inline1, and inline0 encapsulated lookup-table scans while still allowing the compiler to produce the desired inlined structure.
Used explicit loop bounds matching data domains, including 0x42, 0x19, 0xB, 11, and ARRAY_SIZE for recovered local tables.
Used exact integer literal thresholds for unlock/progress checks, such as 0x11940, 0x7530, 0xAFC8, 0x7A120, and 0xF4240, while replacing better-known semantic values with constants.
Used memzero on exact recovered object sizes, including sizeof(lbl_8046DBD8) and the known 0x4D8 size for an unresolved gmMainLib_8015CCE4 return object.
Refined integer widths to match codegen, including u16 argument for gm_80172898, u8 arguments/returns for CPU/character selection helpers, u32 time/counter fields, and u32 return for gm_80181A24.
review_feedback: No human review comments or PR body were present in the slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched-code improvement for GALE01: 41.53% total matched code, +0.11%, +4120 bytes.
The bot listed 26 new fully matched gm_16F1 functions and 3 additional near-matches, which is the primary external validation available in this PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that complete fn_80173098, gm_80173754, or gm_801736E8 after their 99% near-matches in PR 1901.
Search for future refinements or renames of lbl_8046DBD8_t and its fields x0-x8.
Search for the TODO about creating the 0x4D8 struct returned by gmMainLib_8015CCE4.
Check whether lbl_803B7AD0_t, lbl_803D6450_t, or lbl_803D646C_t receive semantic names in later game-mode cleanup.
Look for follow-up header/type changes to gmm_retval_EDBC, gmm_retval_ED98, Unk1PData.xC, and gmMainLib_8015D804.
